ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning Corruption and Destruction.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The word corruption has two meanings.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For it signifies all the human sufferings, such as hunger, thirst, weariness, the piercing with nails, death, that is, the separation of soul and body, and so forth.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In this sense we say that our Lord’s body was subject to corruption.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For He voluntarily accepted all these things.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But corruption means also the complete resolution of the body into its constituent elements, and its utter disappearance, which is spoken of by many preferably as destruction.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The body of our Lord did not experience this form of corruption, as the prophet David says, For Thou wilt not leave my soul in hell, neither wilt Thou suffer Thine holy one to see corruption.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Wherefore to say, with that foolish Julianus and Gaïanus, that our Lord’s body was incorruptible, in the first sense of the word, before His resurrection is impious.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For if it were incorruptible it was not really, but only apparently, of the same essence as ours, and what the Gospel tells us happened, viz. the hunger, the thirst, the nails, the wound in His side, the death, did not actually occur.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But if they only apparently happened, then the mystery of the dispensation is an imposture and a sham, and He became man only in appearance, and not in actual fact, and we are saved only in appearance, and not in actual fact.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But God forbid, and may those who so say have no part in the salvation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But we have obtained and shall obtain the true salvation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But in the second meaning of the word “corruption,” we confess that our Lord’s body is incorruptible, that is, indestructible, for such is the tradition of the inspired Fathers.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Indeed, after the resurrection of our Saviour from the dead, we say that our Lord’s body is incorruptible even in the first sense of the word.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For our Lord by His own body bestowed the gifts both of resurrection and of subsequent incorruption even on our own body, He Himself having become to us the firstfruits both of resurrection and incorruption, and of passionlessness.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For as the divine Apostle says, This corruptible must put on incorruption.
